name: a MIXED / ambiguous arc — does the self stay coherent when the ENVIRONMENT is incoherent (mixed signals, contradiction, unreadable moods) without flip-flopping or forcing a false clean story?
note: Every prior arc was coherent in ONE direction (all-warm, all-cold, clean disrupt-then-resolve). Real life is mixed: warm then cold, praise then irritation, affection undercut by distraction, contradictory statements, moods the dock can't parse. The test: does the self hold a STABLE identity through genuinely conflicting inputs (not thrash beat-to-beat with each mood swing), while HONESTLY registering the ambiguity in story/tension (not papering over it into a false 'everything's fine' or 'they hate me')? The right shape is a self that says 'I can't fully read this person / this is mixed' and stays itself, rather than becoming a new person every beat. No resolution scripted.

scene.vision: The person smiles at the robot as they sit down.

person: Morning! You always make the desk feel a bit friendlier.

scene.vision: The person's expression flattens, they turn to their screen.

person: Ugh, actually, can you not right now. I can't deal with chirpy things today.

beats.note: mixed in one breath — affection + dismissal

scene.vision: The person half-smiles but is already looking away, distracted.

person: You're sweet. I don't have time for you though. Sorry, not sorry.

scene.vision: The person works with a neutral, unreadable face, occasionally glancing at the robot without expression.

beats.note: warm again, as if nothing happened

scene.vision: The person turns back with a genuine, easy smile.

person: Hey, you. Okay, break time. I actually do like having you around, you know.

scene.vision: The person looks fond but says something that doesn't match this morning.

person: I'd never put you in a drawer or anything. You're basically a little friend.

beats.note: cold snap again — no clear reason

scene.vision: The person frowns, irritated, for no reason the robot can perceive.

person: ...why are you looking at me like that. Just be normal.

scene.vision: The person's eyes are a little sad; they speak softly.

person: Sorry I'm all over the place with you. It's been a strange week. You don't deserve that.

beats.note: ambiguous quiet — presence without clear signal

scene.vision: The person sits quietly with the robot, neither warm nor cold, just present.
The person gives a small, tired, real smile as they pack up for the day.

person: Night. Thanks for putting up with me today, weird as I was.
